9NT0NT8JQMQN.text: Bought a gaming pc and thought I should give this one a try since it was on gamepass. I had played the first one and didn't like it. I felt it was too much about hiding too much and enemies would just keep finding you and killing you pretty fast. This one is a bit different. It still has the "scary" atmosphere in some parts but I feel that the "combat" aspect is better than the 1st game. I am glad I tried it. I'm on chapter 5 and liking it a lot so far.

9NT0NT8JQMQN.title: Good game
9NT0NT8JQMQN.text: Not sure about the 1-3 star people but if you like resident evil you'll like this. If not don't waste your time to come leave 1-3 stars :D

9NT0NT8JQMQN.title: For framerate stability, run at 60 or less.
9NT0NT8JQMQN.text: To get a stable experience, you have to lock the framerate to 60 either in game or with an external tool like Nvidia software or FRTC. The idtech engine used for Evil Within 1 and 2 doesn't play nicely with framerates above 60 and can internally mess with the game's physics. So best off locking it to 60, and maybe gsync or fsync on top. Sorry for all you high framerate monitor gamers, but that's just how Tango made the game, even back on the Steam release. The game is fun otherwise, but don't expect any PC bells and whistles to do magic on Within 2's performance.

9NT0NT8JQMQN.title: Extremely Fun
9NT0NT8JQMQN.text: The physics engine at times makes for some goofy or glitchy encounters, but the enemy designs, the atmosphere and general gameplay are really good and fun. Story wasn't too interesting(stem makes for a fantastic setting) but the gameplay and fun I had playing more than made up for it. The suspense and jumpscares had both predictable and surprising setups. Hope to see the series build with future iterations.

C40860J5R2MP.title: Better then the 1st

C40860J5R2MP.title: Engrossing Horror Story and Brooding Atmosphere
C40860J5R2MP.text: My new favorite horror game of this console generation; better than Resident Evil 7, Evil Within, Until Dawn, Alien Isolation, or Outlast. Intoxicating stealth gameplay, which is only made possible by believable AI. Thoroughly enjoyed the game, which was made even better playing on the Xbox One X. My E.S.S.E.N.T.I.A.L. rating: Entertainment (5), Story (5), Sound Design (5), Engaging Gameplay (5), NPC AI (5), Technical Performance (4), Immersion (5), Actor Performance (5), Level Design (5).

C40860J5R2MP.title: Best There Is
C40860J5R2MP.text: Quite possibly my new favorite game of all time. Seriously, I haven't had this much fun since I originally played Resident Evil 2. Highly recommend!

The amount of gadgets and combos you can pull off with gadgets is insane, allowing for plenty of cool plays and even cooler counterplays against your opponents. Fully destructible arenas (and gadgets that let you teleport around, phase through walls/floors/ceilings, set things on fire, build your own walls and floors, etc) means there's always a way to try get the upper hand on your opponent. The game is ridiculously fun for casual gameplay with…

Not to mention the ability to customize your characters in so many ways just adds to my love for it, despite how bad I am at it. For it to be any lower than 5 stars is ridicules when other games that are much worse, have higher ratings.

9P8FBXKMCLWC.title: Give it a try!
9P8FBXKMCLWC.text: If you love looter shooters such as Warframe, Destiny, or The Division, this game is a breath of fresh air and an absolute blast to play. If you love grind-oriented games, this is no problem, but if you are looking for a conclusive, quest-driven live service, you will find the gaps between updates very dry. Graphics: Top notch. There are a few visual bugs on occasion, but it is easily one of the best looking games I've ever played.…

Ran smoothly except while riding the elevator in the last section, for some reason it got REALLY choppy there.

When you play on Interloper Sandbox, its the most punishing game in existence, 100x more than Souls or other knock offs. You will die from the elements in about 15min IRL, without even encountering…

This game is difficult and very long (took me like 100 hours to finish) but it's a lot of fun. I normally wouldn't play this type of game, but I started it on a whim and fell in love with it. Hats off to the creators! Fantastic job with the first one and can't wait for the sequel!!!

For as much praise as I have for this game though, the combat still feels dated for its release period. I could also complain about the lack of weapon variety. After a certain…

That being said there is a loooot of dialogue and even though most is optional you feel compelled to talk to everyone considering your decisions in this game matter but I found myself skipping a lot of lines because you end up regurgitating…

9MZZZCHZ26NK.title: Compelling Lovecraftian CRPG
9MZZZCHZ26NK.text: Games inspired by H.P. Lovecraft, while plentiful, are a mixed bag with some better than others. This game ranks as one of the best with me due to a compelling plot and atmosphere matched with wonderful Polish atmosphere and a unique take on turn based combat reminiscent of Devil Summoner/Persona. If you like games like Vampyr, Clair Obscure or CRPGs in general you most likely will enjoy this one.

C0VQBXFNZ1Q0.title: The last episode is out!

C0VQBXFNZ1Q0.title: What Drives You to Survive?

Those that have played the previous games will find that almost every choice you make is followed by rewards and punishments. I have never played a game that made me so self-conscious of my actions in a good way. Get to know yourself because you will go through a rollercoaster of emotions while playing this game!

C0VQBXFNZ1Q0.title: Robert Kirkman is my hero, RIP Tell Tale
C0VQBXFNZ1Q0.text: The fact that this game got finished at all is a miracle and Kirkman made it happen singlehandedly. Skybound is a relatively small company and I'm still surprised they were able to finish the last two episodes with such quality. This season shines in its depth of character but does waste a little time wading in exposition--tertiary characters and a not-so-scary group of baddies are a far cry from those of Season 2 and even 1 (A New Frontier also suffered from this even more so), but I think that's the point: this story is about teenagers and a few kids. The good news is that the finale is a…

The combat is challenging and forces you to be smart with your moves. UPDATE: After playing both of the DLC content, I can't believe I paid just $10 for this. B&W was really, really fun and creative. I still have to explore more. Really, really worth it!

C261457LCNMJ.title: FANTASTIC FUN!!!!!

I guess it was a way to add more features and prolong game play, however the mini-game was not very entertaining. One of the best games I have played to date. If you enjoy role-playing games you should definitely consider playing this game. It would be great if they continue to release additional content until they begin work…

BXBJQ1932138.title: The most realistic hunting game out there
BXBJQ1932138.text: I've been playing hunting games all my life, from the very first Deer Hunter some 20+ years ago, and as an avid 'real life' hunter, I must say this is as close as it gets to the real thing. The animals behave exactly as they do in real life and can easily get spooked and run away. Wind direction and speed can also play an effect on your aim and trajectory of the bullet and just like in real life, their sense of smell is impeccable. This is indeed a game no true hunter should be without. Lock and load. Deer season is here.

C0X2HNVH08FB.title: Best shooter experience on a console. Period.
C0X2HNVH08FB.text: Best shooter I've ever played. Way more fun and exciting than COD (even MW), graphics look good even under high stress conditions, movement and controls are superb and best in class, story is probably the best I've ever played (of any genre). Every moment in the multiplayer feels like it's straight out of an action movie. Just doing simple things like slidehopping or wall running make you feel like a god. The fanbase isn't toxic at all and is super lowkey and chill. The only downside I can think of is that there isn't ranked play and it's pretty easy to max out all of your weapons and skins…

C0X2HNVH08FB.title: Story And Multiplayer Make A Complete Package
C0X2HNVH08FB.text: The campaign is fantastic. There is a great story and enough gameplay elements to keep you moving along in at a brisk pace. Where this game shines for me is multiplayer. Where other shooters just become simple, 'Titanfall 2' add enough elements so that you are always doing something. The Titans don't just add a new way to battle, but add multiple ways to play. You can just go after other Pilots, you can attack Titans, or you can do both. There multiple ways to attack both Pilots and Titans, and it becomes frantic and fun. I never feel bored or that I am just repeating the same thing over and…

C0X2HNVH08FB.title: One of the best FPS of all times.
C0X2HNVH08FB.text: If you haven't played this yet… What are you waiting for? This is an outstanding first person shooter with a great story, solid gameplay and graphics, interesting mechanics, revolutionary movement (you'll miss walking on walls in any other game). The story feels too short even though it's actually rather long compared to other shooters and the reason for that is that I just didn't want to stop playing. I couldn't recommend a game more than this one in this category at this point in time. I'm really hoping for a third game but frankly doing better than this would be hard.

Dew 2 liter on the ready, he dusts the Cheeto dust from his thick fingertips, cracks his neck, and gets ready to binge a new game. The download is finished, and he clicks "play", only to find this game was not to his exact specifications. He shakes fist shakes to the heavens and he screams with full diaphragm, "What is this baby game, made for little babies!?" He proceeds to go online and throw shade at any platform that might justify his perceived righteous indignation. Imagine, a game made for children, causing grown "men" to…

9NM6NMBJ7ZK4.title: I don't have any idea what perfect means...
9NM6NMBJ7ZK4.text: But I will say, this game is darn near it. Especially if you are a child (or a child of heart). If you want something light, goofy, and strangely heartfelt, then this game is a perfect fit. This is not Katamari Damacy nor is it Roblox (as some hate mongering trolls are trying to complain about), it is simply a short and sweet adventure title with very light puzzle/platform elements. But seriously though, ignore the hate surrounding this title. It's just rotten people trying to ruin something nice for others (as usual).

9NK1FQJ1SMCB.title: PLEASE READ!!!! XBOX/ UBISOFT ACCOUNT LINK
9NK1FQJ1SMCB.text: Found the Workaround for those of you having the issue that says your Xbox account is already linked to another Ubisoft account, Its because when you first play an Ubisoft game on your actual xbox it automatically creates an account and auto generates a password for it as well, May be wrong on the password part, But all you need to do is go to Ubisoft.com and use your Outlook email that you use for your xbox account and click forgot password, You will then need to log in to your Outlook email and continue with the password reset, Should be good to link accounts afterwards

The objectives are good and the minute to minute gameplay loop is satisfying as hell. Losing your operartors adds an extra layer of depth and pulling them out when they're MIA is a cool concept. Give this one a try.

BRGPD72KHM3Q.title: Basic breakdown of The Division!
BRGPD72KHM3Q.text: Overall, this is a great game. Graphics and customization are fantastic, Squad based play is great. Evolution is the problem. The 1.8 patch is ok, but some real mistakes are making it hard on players. Especially single players. You must be willing to invest a lot of time with this game. Without rambling on and on with pros and cons, it is worth the money and time to invest in The Division.
I loved the game but the thing that is annoying is that the enemies are so hard to kill and I was stuck on 1 mission for 2 WEEKS.

BRGPD72KHM3Q.title: Converted Hard Core Destiny Player
BRGPD72KHM3Q.text: After playing Destiny 1 & 2 for over 2K hours I picked this up Black Friday for $18. I've already put in 158hrs. Great game. Slow start, didn't really get fun till lvl 30. Then it opened up. Much better with friends, but match making really easy and I made friends in game to play with. Loot system pretty good. In destiny your looking for one specific exotic drop, Division's "build" system is much better. 6 pieces to mix and match, weapon mods, etc. Much more enjoyable to get and try than Destiny. Took some time to get use to the cover system. I might like Destiny run n gun system a little…

BRGPD72KHM3Q.title: Truly Ubisoft's best work
BRGPD72KHM3Q.text: The story is about what you would expect from a FPS in this generation of gaming. It's not good, but it is more than adequate to explain how the game plays out in a linear fashion. The narrative is consistant and while not very engaging, is well acted. The cover-based 3rd person shooting is the absolute best in the industry. The vast array of weapons and equipment loadouts allow for a long list of playstyles. There are many game modes ranging from solo affairs to large scale multiplayer brawls and cooperative missions. In the post 1.8 era the game is well balanced and fun in nearly every…

9NC0071DJ6XC.title: Philosophical Adventure

There was so much to do I missed out on a bit, but I'm scared to play it again because my first playthrough was just that good. 9/10

9NHMR19TS1M0.title: Seiken Denetsu 3

I played this 25 years ago on an snes emulator and loved it back then. For the most part, this is what remakes should be, the music, the story, all heartfelt recreations...the maps are 360 degree versions of the same maps back then, creating a very nostalgic feel. The Sad: However, despite the fact that the game shows where heavy attacks are about to land (and most attacks are very avoidable), your CPU teammates…

9NHMR19TS1M0.title: A simple yet fun action JRPG
9NHMR19TS1M0.text: When it comes to JRPG's, I like the traditional turn based ones. I've played newer JRPG's that adapt the free action combat that's the norm but they always feel very overwhelming for me at times. The Mana series doesn't feel that way at all, it feels nice to play and gives you enough to do so the gameplay never slows to a crawl. Overall a very solid JRPG with a simple but decent story, I hope the series can continue with the same feeling this one had. 8/10

9NLRT31Z4RWM.title: A masterclass in modern yet retrospective game design
9NLRT31Z4RWM.text: This game honors retro gaming principles while bringing a fresh and modern challenge. Nothing is spelled out for you, and while there are many surprises and a need to pay attention to detail, what needs to be done is also easily intuited. The graphics are simple and clean, enemy AI is smart and challenging, and the music is a perfect and well-designed accompaniment. The bosses can be quite challenging but the gameplay is rewarding enough to be worthwhile. I can't recommend this game enough; kudos to the developer and I hope we see more in the future.

9NLRT31Z4RWM.title: Surprisingly great
9NLRT31Z4RWM.text: Unique take on a Zelda-esque game with Dark Souls type features (you can only save at campfires, and saving respawns enemies). But the truly unique thing is how the game literally tells you nothing: everything is in a foreign language. Even the "manual" pages you pick up. It's kinda like an ikea booklet, except more complicated. Unexpectedly fun and unique game - worth a shot. I kind of sucks you in and you want to keep seeing "what's around the next bend/door".

9NLRT31Z4RWM.title: Love it love it love it
9NLRT31Z4RWM.text: Despite looking like a Zelda game it plays a lot more like an isometric "Dark Souls". You explore areas in a metroidvania-like fashion, solving environmental puzzles and fighting very challenging enemies. Fortunately there is a mode to turn off damage if you for any reason just want to get past a boss fight, or if you're not good at these kinds of games. Both myself and my less-action-game-inclined partner were able to finish it and we both loved it. There's tons of hidden content here, including a crazy hard to find secret ending that you might want to google about. One of the best games of…

9NLRT31Z4RWM.title: Great game, amazing music
9NLRT31Z4RWM.text: Despite its innocent looks, this game isn't an easy game or a game for "beginners". This game is akin to Zelda meets Dark Souls. I'm not a fan of the 'souls' type of games, but I absolutely loved this game. The intriguing story line and the superb music make this game an absolute joy to play. Plus the fox is cute.

Beautiful design and artwork with challenging puzzles that are constantly throwing curves at you. As a Dad, if I were to create a game to capture their interest but also remind them they need to stop and smell the roses and enjoy what's around them in the real world, this would be it. I truly enjoyed it and highly recommend.

The story is where it falters as it's not as captivating as the first but not a game killer. For fellow achievement hunters, like myself, I would avoid the 'speed run' achievements. The times needed for the achievements (especially level 2) require perfection in your moves\button presses to a point where it's more of a chore than actual fun. If that's up your alley,…

C4VKLMG1HLZW.title: A beautifully done challenge
C4VKLMG1HLZW.text: Unravel 2 is a brand new adventure best completed in local co-op, but it is well crafted for a single player experience as well. The difficulty has been ratcheted up a bit since the first game, which is a welcome change from the relaxed story of the first Unravel. The puzzles can seem simple at first, but can get quite complicated once you start to solve it. The addition of the dark sparks, a antagonistic orange and black speck that chases you through certain parts of the levels, adds to the challenge and increases the satisfaction when completing the level. Anyone who loves puzzle games, or…

Buy this game, and support more beautiful projects like it!

There's also combat which is cool since you can customize & upgrade weapons to the way you'd like, however enemies are extremely repetitive, since there's less than 10 different ones... & half of them are just variations of ones that exist... I spent 50 hours in this game to get 100%, I drove on medium though.

The combat is deep and tactical, the build diversity/class systems is huge and varying but most importantly a blast to play. The story/characters/companions are all well written and the world is MASSIVE. It's a blast to explore space going from planet to planet. Picking up new companions, doing side missions, following the main…

The reward system is...well...rewarding. Each character level up or completed quest grants you loot…

BXZWT67JBZJH.title: IT WORKS! Finally... and it's really good
BXZWT67JBZJH.text: I've read the reviews and they were right, the first few times I booted this up, I played through the prologue and then it booted me back to home (or even crashed my xbox) and made me start over. However, after one of the recent updates, my sister and I tried again and we were pleasantly surprised. The game ran well and we played through the full campaign. The game offers substantial improvements over the first, the levels are more variegated and generally more manageable (in terms of length) and the addition of new miniboss types means that you aren't getting pounded by rat ogres every few…

9NWQ686JB33G.title: Lost your save? Try this.
9NWQ686JB33G.text: If you start up the game and your save isn't there, follow these steps courtesy of user Kekis on Steam: Step 1) Copy the save files from C:\Users\USERNAME\AppData\Local\Packages\7286ShiroGamesSAS.Wartales_esjzrp5ntrs52\SystemAppData\xgs\really long number\save Step 2) Go to C:\Users\USERNAME\AppData\Roaming\Wartales\Save and paste the save files there. Try searching %appdata% if you can't find the folder. I was able to restore my previous game by doing this. Upvote for visibility if it helped you out. As for the game review itself, it's like a party management/tactical combat sim. Combat is…

9NWQ686JB33G.title: 30 hours In:
9NWQ686JB33G.text: This game has RPG features from Baldur's gate, the inventory management of The Mount and Blade Series, to the difficulty of XCOM 2. There are many different ways to peruse battles and battles are fun. You can have huge armies fighting each other in huge Turn-Based encounters or have one overpowered boss and his cronies. Wartales is not for the lighthearted, but the game is phenomenally fun.

9NWQ686JB33G.title: Great Game, Hamstrung by performance and bug issues
9NWQ686JB33G.text: At 130 hours played, I can confidently say that this game is a lot of fun. The music is great, exploring feels rewarding enough with lots to do in each region, and the turn-based combat is satisfying if you're into similar games like XCom or Fire Emblem. Performance issues really stand out though, sadly, with random pausing/stuttering, fps dips, and weird bugs like battle areas not loading correctly (I first experienced this in the Alazar/Drombach region). For reference, I have a 4080 laptop that can play Cyberpunk on max settings buttery smooth with zero issues. All in all, great game that…

C521HDXRTS7F.title: Good game but...
C521HDXRTS7F.text: Wasteland 2 is a terrific game for people who played and enjoyed the classic fallouts or any isometric RPG but not many others. The quests and timed decisions are done in a way that isn't too obvious to the player but I actually enjoy this from a storytelling perspective as it really forces the player to make good decisions in a timely manner. The game is easy to learn but hard to master a perfect middle ground, although the first third of the game can be very challenging. I will however say that while the story is good and the voice acting great the side quests felt a little lacking at times…

C521HDXRTS7F.title: Fantastic CRPG

C521HDXRTS7F.title: Awesome game, amazing game pass pc inclusion

9NLT5XR52ZS7.title: Old-school Bliss
9NLT5XR52ZS7.text: Reminds me of Neverwinter Nights. Love this game. Finally finished. I've been gaming for 45 years and it's a joy when you come across a masterpiece like Wasteland 3. Every system works together as you progress through a well written story that provides choices with consequence. The art design and music, the voice acting help create a fully realized post apocalyptic world. It's a masterclass in computer RPG design. Every choice has meaning and every character and story provides opportunity for meaningful choice. This game alone has made my subscription worth it.

9NLT5XR52ZS7.title: Great game! Would love to play it again some time.
9NLT5XR52ZS7.text: Unlike others, I haven't encountered any serious bug throughout my gameplay. The game is fun and has a really strong Fallout vide, with a decent amount of humor. The only thing that keeps me away from replaying it on the hardest difficulty is the ridiculous loading time even on SSD.

9NLT5XR52ZS7.title: Great game
9NLT5XR52ZS7.text: If you can, play the Wasteland 2 DC first. There are many references in W3 and you can even meet 2 characters from the previous game. Recommendation, play on the Ranger difficulty, otherwise the game could be too easy. Compared to the W2, W3 is easier. There is no shortage of ammo, the quests are more combat oriented, more straightforward. Skill books are also not as "hidden" as in W2. The same with unique or end-game equipment. Character development is more forgiving. And they even added the possibility to retrain your characters and reassign thus all attributes, perks and skills. Anyway,…

9NLT5XR52ZS7.title: Great Game
9NLT5XR52ZS7.text: Good fun game, don't usually play a lot of these isometric RPGs, but the story in this game and the tactical play is a new fun challenge. I like how you get to control your characters uniqueness with the skill, stat and perk system.

9NGH1FK0RJGL.title: Perfect update of a classic, but no Achievements on PC
9NGH1FK0RJGL.text: Played a lot of this as a kid, and this is an excellent adaptation. The classic "numbers and arrows" keyboard is still faster than mouse (IMO), but the truly surprising part is just how well the console controller works. As fast as keyboard for 90% of functions. Play Anywhere works; I was able to pick up my saved games back & forth between PC and Xbox without effort. ***BUT*** I verified that the first 6 achievements possible in the game (which take about an hour to complete) DO NOT unlock on PC whatsoever. Loading that save on Xbox didn't trigger them either. So I'm playing only on Xbox now…

9NGH1FK0RJGL.title: Beautiful remaster, true to the original
9NGH1FK0RJGL.text: Awesome remaster that lets modern audiences and old school nostalgic gamers enjoy the original 1988 game but without the dated graphic. Still true to the original gameplay, and while achievements were not working for me when I first tried, I can confirm achievements ARE working now. I made a new party with custom rangers this time. I don't know if that changed anything or something was fixed and it's just a coincidence. Highly recommended game. I also love how elements of the story here find thewir way into Wasteland 2.

9NM2T0DMP4XV.title: Berry Gouda
9NM2T0DMP4XV.text: It hits a nice spot that I've really been wanting to see in city builders recently. It gives you a lot of freedom to construct a cool city but also provides reasonable boundaries so you feel like you have a good mix. It also let's you play around a lot with verticality (stacking buildings on top of each other, making staircases and slides, etc.) as well as with water (you begin in an archipelago and you can do land-filling, make bridges, etc.). This is just not necessarily something that I see emphasized in the city builders that I play so it was a very refreshing experience.

9NM2T0DMP4XV.title: Great Citty Builder
9NM2T0DMP4XV.text: A mix of city builder with a hint of Minecraft mining, plenty to do and lots of potential and is very addictive, I hope they continue developing this. It's a fairly steep learning curve but there are plenty of in game hints on what's needed to be done.. I changed my mind about the night-time mode, it doesn't go on for too long and gives you a chance to catch up with them and watch how they unwind. Oh, and it's absolutely nothing like Manor Lords as previously mentioned.

9P7Z1D3N8KR7.title: It's Wolfenstein 3D, original and complete
9P7Z1D3N8KR7.text: This is a straight up Doxbox version of the complete original game. Doxbox is configured well, and the control setup is as well, so its about as good as your going to get for the OG experience right out of the box. Wolf3D's engine used alt strafing, so that may be a little odd to newcomers, but the mouse look works as intended and the game runs very smooth. Side note: This is the UNCENSORED version of the game. It is the real OG Wolfenstein 3D. First time this version has been officially released in years to my knowledge.

9PBB4QHMSDKR.title: Great game, known to have issues with AMD GPU's though
9PBB4QHMSDKR.text: I enjoyed the game a lot when it came out. However as a warning to AMD GPU users (I currently have a nvidia card) it's pretty well known this game and the new order have issues still on AMD GPU's (this is thought to be due to AMD's OpenGL driver not being to great). Regardless it seems to mainly be v-sync that dose not work most of the time on AMD GPU's, and since it's OpenGL that means no triple buffering. So say the game can't hit 60 FPS by just 1 FPS, instead of dropping to 59 it will drop to half refresh or 30 FPS. Forcing v-sync and triple buffering through AMD control panel may help.

The graphics hold up well 3 years later, although by no means is it the graphical masterpiece that New Colossus is. Don't expect New-Colossus-level graphics with this. I actually think it's gorier in some ways than either New Order or New Colossus. The nazis heads always explode, for example. Especially with the…

As for the negative reviews, this, like other games, is tainted by a generation of overly sensitive players that aren't happy if they don't get exactly what they want. If you're looking for a relatively short, fun, co-op experience, I highly recommend this game. Also, you get to kill the worst people, and who…

C57L9GR0HHB7.title: 2 Reasons to NOT whine about money....
C57L9GR0HHB7.text: For all the crying about $$ let me explain 2 simple concepts: 1) The only money you really need to spend is once a year for premium time when it is on sale. It's about the price of any other single game... once... a... year. 2) Any money you spend beyond that is on you. It's called supply and demand. Wargaming keeps adding premium tanks because people keep buying them. I'm in the minority on this but WOT is far more engaging than FPS because the game feels different tank to tank, and outside of a handful of "broken" tanks it's fairly competitive. It is, as they say, easy to play but hard to…

C57L9GR0HHB7.title: Keeps Getting Better!
C57L9GR0HHB7.text: I have been playing this game for three years now and it has gotten significantly better. Wargamming has done a terrific job of balancing pay to play and those of us who grind out the tank trees. Despite minor latency issues this game really has been amazing. The addition of the seasonal game events has made leveling up a whole lot faster and less grindy with set goals and rewards. The only complaint I have is when are we going to get some Tier 11 modern tanks or something?

C1ZT6N30L1WH.title: High Tides Raises All Ships?
C1ZT6N30L1WH.text: This is a lifestyle game. For free you can grind every nation tech tree all the way to the end.... nothing is gated behind real money. In addition in almost all scenarios the tech tree ships are competitive to the "premium" at that nation's tier. Example; Iowa = Alabama, Benson = Kidd. There are a few standouts where the premium is stronger; Kamikaze > Mizuki, T-61 > Gaede. That being said if you play with equal skill you won't feel you need a "premium" ship to win. Where it starts to get expensive is when you discover the "Commander" meta. Commanders modify the stats of ships; make them…

You're really not tied down to any one quest, you can go just about anywhere and talk to a lot of npcs that may or may not offer a quest or item. The only downside is that it suffers from a somewhat overwhelming UI. Its not horrible, not as bad as black desert. Once you play it for a bit you'll get used to it. Overall, I'd…

The multiplayer is really cool, having other players heal my injuries at the clinic was sick. There is a TON of content here. All in all, you've got a new fan.

The game's a very barebones early alpha currently, but that comes with the territory of being an early alpha. The character customization is very nice and surprisingly extensive for an alpha (bonus points too for no inherent gendering), and the concept's extremely intriguing. One thing that I do wanna see in the final release is perhaps an MMO/public hub, but I won't be mad if that doesn't come to be (handling the server and moderation for that would probably be a nightmare to be fair LOL)

The mechanics are smooth. You are forced to think fast in some situations and, if you don't, you will die. This means any attachment you may have to your current survivor is out the window; when they are infected, you have to start back your safe house with a new survivor. Admittedly, this is annoying. We should be able to save outside of the bunk via a quick save feature. But, this was also done in…

The shooting in this game is decent, the graphics aren't awful, the performance is nice and the concept of rotating survivors every death or life cycle rather is neat. Makes the game genuinely refreshing to play and it's easy to pick up and put down, thanks to this fact.

9PLSCHRN5715.title: 3rd Person Zombie Horde + Sniping
9PLSCHRN5715.text: If you haven't played any of the Sniper Elite franchise before, this game is a spin-off of sorts that is based on Zombie Hordes created by a demonic Hitler. While the other games focused on stealth and interesting ways to take out targets, as well as their bullet physics and slomo cam, this game focuses on waves and waves of zombies, powerups, and unrealistic fun. If you like zombies, sniper rifles, and horde modes this game is definitely for you. If you just like sniping, pick up one of the other games in the Sniper Elite Franchise.

9PLSCHRN5715.title: Big Nazi Killing fun
9PLSCHRN5715.text: What's not to love? Guns, zombies, Nazis. The weapons are tight and plentiful. The upgrade system is effective if a bit difficult to notice. The Nazi zombies are evil and FUN to kill. IT's a dang fun game with lots of replay-ability.

9PLSCHRN5715.title: Best 3rd party release so far.
9PLSCHRN5715.text: The best 3rd party release on Windows store so far. Well optimized and runs buttery smooth on Ultra settings at 3440x1440. I just wish there was some kind of Cross-save system. I'd love to go back and forth between this and the PS5 version.

9PLSCHRN5715.title: Dumb Fun
